assessment_items.question_content:
**Raphael's *School of Athens* shows how the Catholic world rejected the classical world during the High Renaissance.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f1190ddf7b01df0771562bf8a2c260f2.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Raphael's *School of Athens* shows how the Catholic world embraced the classical world during the High Renaissance.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of Raphael's *School of Athens*?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f1190ddf7b01df0771562bf8a2c260f2.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The two central figures in the painting are Plato and Aristotle, Greek philosophers who influenced the ideas of the High Renaissance.**

choices.content: The architecture in this painting is more Roman than Greek.
The scene is a fabrication, showing Greek philosophers who lived at  different times  together at a single gathering.
Bramante's design for the new St. Peter's Basilica influenced the architecture in the painting.
The two central figures in the painting are Christ and St. Peter.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The gestures of Plato and Aristotle in the painting reference the ideas of these philosophers. Plato points to the heavens, as his writings dealt with intangible heavenly things. Aristotle points down because he was interested in observable, tangible, earthly things.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ebb5ddc92951ba0e518787a620ee32dc.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The gestures of Plato and Aristotle in the painting reference the ideas of these philosophers. Plato points to the heavens, as his writings dealt with intangible heavenly things. Aristotle points down because he was interested in observable, tangible, earthly things.**
